name: KPT
source_reference: official public framework source URL required
allowed_use_boundary: Use only as provisional intake evidence derived from public LinkedIn positioning and captured descriptive text.
Do not treat the LinkedIn profile or captured description as a complete technical specification.
Do not treat this manifest as certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority.
claims: KPT is publicly positioned as a runtime decision-governance boundary before downstream consequence.
KPT states that AI output should not become authority by default.
KPT is a candidate external-framework comparison target for output progression, influence, and execution boundaries.
non_claims: No official versioned KPT specification, repository, schema, API, or implementation source is recorded.
No framework-wide validation, certification, endorsement, or production interoperability is claimed.
A KPT decision state does not establish StegVerse execution authority or commit-time admissibility.

transition_table_mapping.framework_identity: KPT, publicly associated with founder Kristian Jõgi
transition_table_mapping.source_reference: official public framework source URL required
transition_table_mapping.allowed_use_boundary: source-blocked runtime decision-boundary intake and terminology comparison only
transition_table_mapping.claims: candidate output progression boundary before influence, persistent knowledge, approval support, or execution
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no official technical source verification, certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority
transition_table_mapping.input_artifact_type: AI-generated candidate output and proposed downstream consequence
transition_table_mapping.output_artifact_type: provisional decision state, trace evidence, and controlled-route instruction
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: unknown until official source is provided; public text distinguishes candidate output from authority
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: traceable evidence is publicly stated; official schema and sufficiency rules are unavailable
transition_table_mapping.policy_or_rule_model: explicit boundary decision before progression; official policy representation unavailable
transition_table_mapping.delegation_model: unknown until official source is provided
transition_table_mapping.decision_or_result_model: explicit boundary decision before downstream consequence; official result vocabulary unavailable
transition_table_mapping.receipt_or_trace_model: trace before trust is publicly stated; official receipt or trace format unavailable
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: captured public positioning reconstructs why intake is provisional and source-blocked
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: candidate decision evidence may precede independent standing and authority reconstruction
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: runtime transition boundary, evidence posture, controlled downstream route, and commit-time admissibility comparison
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: official source missing, source version missing, result vocabulary undefined, trace schema unavailable, authority inheritance, or endorsement overclaim

SPE_overlap.standing_boundary: KPT output cannot establish standing by itself
SPE_overlap.authority_evaluation: must be reconstructed independently at commit time
SPE_overlap.receipt_reconstruction: source-blocked intake state is reconstructable; runtime receipt format is unavailable

StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap.transition_table: provisional mapping pending official KPT source
StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap.documentation_surface: wiki reference requires official-source upgrade before technical compatibility claims

fail_closed_conditions: official public framework source is missing
official decision vocabulary and trace schema are unavailable
delegation and authority semantics are undefined
any certification, endorsement, execution-authority, or commit-time-authority claim appears
